R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com.

When you click the Knit button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# ============================================================
# Step 1. Setup: Load Packages & Data
# ============================================================
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, janitor, lubridate, skimr, caret, glmnet, 
  broom, ggplot2, knitr, kableExtra, arrow, corrplot,
  rpart, rpart.plot, randomForest, ranger
)

# Load the full 186-column Parquet file
flights_raw <- open_dataset("C:/Users/ZyrIr/OneDrive/Desktop/flights_canonical_2019.parquet") %>%
  collect() %>%
  clean_names()

# ============================================================
# Step 2. Master Cleaning & Feature Engineering Pipeline
# ============================================================
# This creates a single 'source of truth' for all subsequent analysis
df_final <- flights_raw %>%
  # 1. Filter for your target NYC hubs
  filter(origin == "BWI", dest %in% c("JFK", "EWR")) %>%
  
  # 2. SELECT: Include every column needed for Models, Hubs, and Holidays
  select(
    flight_date, reporting_airline, tail_number, arr_delay, dep_delay, 
    carrier_delay, weather_delay, taxi_out, dep_hour_local, 
    day_of_week, month, dep_temp_c, dep_wind_speed_m_s, 
    dep_ceiling_height_m, prev_arr_delay, turnaround_minutes, 
    aircraft_leg_number_day, crs_elapsed_time, actual_elapsed_time, distance
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. CLEAN: Handle NAs (The fix for na.fail errors)
  mutate(
    across(c(prev_arr_delay, weather_delay, carrier_delay), ~replace_na(., 0)),
    month_day = format(as.Date(flight_date), "%m-%d")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(arr_delay), !is.na(dep_delay)) %>%
  
  # 4. FEATURE ENGINEERING: Strategy, Holidays, and Plane Size Proxy
  mutate(
    is_delayed        = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No")),
    is_weekend        = as.factor(ifelse(day_of_week %in% c(6, 7), "Yes", "No")),
    is_rush_hour      = as.factor(ifelse(dep_hour_local %in% c(7, 8, 16, 17, 18), "Yes", "No")),
    reporting_airline = as.factor(reporting_airline),
    buffer            = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time,
    is_holiday        = as.factor(case_when(
      month_day >= "11-22" & month_day <= "12-02" ~ "Holiday", # Thanksgiving
      month_day >= "12-19" | month_day <= "01-05" ~ "Holiday", # Winter
      TRUE ~ "Non-Holiday"
    )),
    # PLANE SIZE PROXY: Feature Aggregation/Heuristic Classification
    plane_size_score = case_when(
      distance > 1500 ~ "Large",
      distance > 600  ~ "Medium",
      TRUE            ~ "Small"
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# Step 3. Descriptive Visuals & Business Intelligence
# ============================================================

# 1. Correlation Plot (Numeric only)
df_final %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% drop_na() %>% 
  cor() %>% corrplot(method = "color", addCoef.col = "black", tl.cex = 0.7, title="Predictor Matrix")

# 2. Scheduling Honesty (Lollipop Chart)
all_airline_buffers <- flights_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(crs_elapsed_time), !is.na(actual_elapsed_time)) %>%
  mutate(buffer_mins = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time) %>%
  group_by(reporting_airline) %>%
  summarise(avg_buffer = mean(buffer_mins, na.rm = TRUE), count = n()) %>%
  filter(count > 100)

ggplot(all_airline_buffers, aes(x = reorder(reporting_airline, avg_buffer), y = avg_buffer)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = reporting_airline, yend = 0), color = "grey") +
  geom_point(size = 4, aes(color = avg_buffer)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightpink", high = "purple") + coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Airline Strategy: The Buffer Rankings", y="Avg Buffer (min)")

# ============================================================
# Step 4. Tree-Based Machine Learning (NO NA ERRORS)
# ============================================================

# Prepare the cleaned-subset for modeling
df_model <- df_final %>%
  select(is_delayed, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, 
         aircraft_leg_number_day, plane_size_score, is_holiday, is_weekend) %>%
  drop_na() # <--- CRITICAL FIX: Removes any remaining missing values

# 1. Decision Tree (The 'Logic Map')
tree_fit <- rpart(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_model, method = "class")
rpart.plot(tree_fit, extra = 101, main = "Decision Tree: BWI-NYC Delay Logic")

# 2. Random Forest (The 'Powerhouse')
rf_fit <- randomForest(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_model, ntree = 100, importance = TRUE)
varImpPlot(rf_fit, main = "Variable Importance: What truly causes NYC delays?")

# 3. National Scale Model (Ranger)
df_full_national <- flights_raw %>%
  select(arr_delay, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, aircraft_leg_number_day, distance) %>%
  mutate(is_delayed = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No"))) %>%
  drop_na()

full_rf_ranger <- ranger(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_full_national, 
                         importance = "impurity", num.trees = 100,
                         num.threads = parallel::detectCores() - 1)
## Growing trees.. Progress: 51%. Estimated remaining time: 29 seconds.
print(full_rf_ranger)
## Ranger result
## 
## Call:
##  ranger(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_full_national, importance = "impurity",      num.trees = 100, num.threads = parallel::detectCores() -          1) 
## 
## Type:                             Classification 
## Number of trees:                  100 
## Sample size:                      7288028 
## Number of independent variables:  6 
## Mtry:                             2 
## Target node size:                 1 
## Variable importance mode:         impurity 
## Splitrule:                        gini 
## OOB prediction error:             0.00 %
